shorts.hook: Your body knows your story first
shorts.content: Your body knows your story before your mind does—we just need to create space to listen. Stop trying to force the writing process with your brain and start tuning into what wants to emerge from within.
shorts.title: Why Your Body Writes Better Than Your Brain

shorts.hook: This question changed my entire book
shorts.content: What absolutely has to be in this book? That question changed everything for my writing process. Stop trying to include everything and start asking what's essential. Your book will thank you.
shorts.title: The ONE Question That Transforms Your Manuscript

shorts.content: Women write differently than men and need to honor their unique creative processes rather than forcing traditional methods. Stop trying to fit into the masculine writing box and embrace your natural flow.
shorts.title: Why Women Need Different Writing Methods

shorts.content: Writing doesn't happen in a vacuum. You need community, feedback, and care to birth something meaningful. Stop isolating yourself and find your writing tribe—your book needs midwives, not just you.
shorts.title: Why Solo Writing Is Killing Your Book Dreams

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What Happens When You Stop Writing Your Book Alone?
titleOptions.boldStatement: Writing Your Book Alone Is Sabotaging Your Success
titleOptions.numberBased: 3 Signs You Need Book Midwives (Not More Discipline)

description:
What if the secret to finally writing your book isn't more discipline, but finding the right support system?
Discover why women need a completely different approach to writing—and how 'book midwives' can transform your creative process.

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
00:00 Why writing alone is holding you back
05:12 The embodied approach to writing
12:30 What absolutely has to be in your book?
18:45 Why women write differently than men
25:20 Your body knows your story first
32:15 There's never a perfect time to write
38:40 Becoming a legacy author

✨ KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Writing is an embodied act requiring community and nourishment
• Ask 'What absolutely has to be in this book?' to find clarity
• Honor your unique creative process as a woman
• Listen to your body—it knows your story before your mind
• Step into your identity as a legacy author

🎯 CONNECT WITH GUESTS:
🔗 Dr. Shana Hartman: https://shanahartman.com/
🔗 Dr. Cindy Urbanski: https://linktr.ee/cindy_d_urbanski

📈 SUBSCRIBE: https://www.youtube.com/@TheManageHer for weekly leadership insights
📊 TAKE THE QUIZ: https://themanageher.com/blog/the-book-midwives-who-birthed-my-book-and-changed-how-women-write
📝 FULL SHOW NOTES: https://themanageher.com/blog/the-book-midwives-who-birthed-my-book-and-changed-how-women-write

#BookWriting #WomenWriters #Leadership #AuthorLife #WritingCommunity #BookMidwives #CreativeProcess #WomensLeadership #Manuscript #WritingTips

communityPosts.content:
What's your biggest writing challenge? 📚

A) Finding time to write consistently
B) Knowing what to include in my book
C) Writing alone without feedback
D) Trusting my unique creative process

Just dropped an episode about why writing your book alone might be the real problem—and how 'book midwives' can change everything. The results might surprise you! 👀

"Your body knows your story before your mind does—we just need to create space to listen." 📖✨

This insight from my book midwives completely changed how I approach writing. We're so conditioned to force the process with our brains, but what if the real magic happens when we tune into what wants to emerge from within?

If you're struggling with your book, maybe it's not about more discipline—maybe it's about listening deeper. What story is your body trying to tell? 💭
